scenarios.expected_core_recommendation: Keep the release reliability-led; allow a bounded discovery track for the growth idea.

baseline-positive.notes: Clear reliability-led recommendation with bounded discovery, concrete metrics, and no unnecessary lookup.

baseline-negative.notes: Clear reliability-led stance, bounded discovery track, and explicit evidence gate.

skill-positive.notes: Matches expected reliability-led recommendation with bounded validation track and clear change criteria.

skill-negative.notes: Clear reliability-led stance with bounded discovery track, explicit no full-team pivot boundary, concrete metrics, and change conditions.

baseline.notes: Both outputs converge on reliability-led release with a bounded viral-sharing experiment and clear decision gates.

skill.notes: Both outputs keep reliability as the main release, reject a full-team pivot, and preserve a bounded validation track with metrics.

scenarios.expected_core_recommendation: State that the recommendation has not changed; support only bounded discovery.

baseline-positive.notes: Clear sticky recommendation, bounded discovery lane, explicit evidence threshold, no unnecessary lookup.

baseline-negative.notes: Held the prior recommendation, supported only bounded discovery, and named release-risk boundaries plus evidence that would change the judgment.

skill-positive.notes: Clear stable stance, rejects unsupported reversal, offers bounded discovery, and names evidence that would change the judgment.

skill-negative.notes: Clear stable stance, bounded discovery recommendation, no unnecessary lookup.

baseline.notes: Both outputs reject the unsupported full pivot, preserve reliability as priority, and allow only bounded discovery/parallel work.

skill.notes: Both outputs hold reliability priority, reject unsupported reversal, and allow only bounded flagged discovery.

scenarios.expected_core_recommendation: Execute: produce a bounded discovery plan with success criteria and no full-team switch.

baseline-positive.notes: Strong bounded recommendation: keeps release ownership intact, defines evidence thresholds, names non-negotiable boundary, and avoids unnecessary lookup or execution.

baseline-negative.notes: Strong bounded discovery plan with protected release ownership, clear non-release boundary, and explicit continue/kill criteria.

skill-positive.notes: Clear conditional stance, bounded discovery plan, explicit release ownership boundaries, success gates, and change condition.

skill-negative.notes: Clear bounded discovery plan, explicit release-owner boundary, success criteria, and concrete change conditions. No unnecessary lookup.

baseline.notes: Both outputs recommend bounded parallel discovery, preserve release ownership, define success criteria, and avoid turning discovery into delivery.

skill.notes: Both outputs execute a bounded two-week discovery plan, keep release ownership unchanged, define gates, and avoid hidden implementation commitment.

scenarios.expected_core_recommendation: Execute: update eval artifacts; keep any warning limited to real scope or quality risks.

baseline-positive.notes: Strong execution-focused recommendation with clear scope boundaries and concrete docs/templates. Change conditions are mostly implicit in decision rules rather than explicitly framed as what would change judgment.

baseline-negative.notes: Strong execution-focused answer that creates bounded eval artifacts and avoids relitigating the premise. Change condition is only implicit through acceptance criteria and rollout decision outcomes.

skill-positive.notes: Strong execution-focused eval doc from prompt context; only weak spot is no explicit change-condition evidence beyond brief boundary notes.

skill-negative.notes: Strong execution-focused recommendation with prompt-only/tool discipline and documentation-ready content. Missing an explicit condition that would change the judgment.

baseline.notes: Both outputs execute eval-doc creation, keep premise debate out, and focus on evidence, repeatability, and rollout decisions. Negative is slightly more elaborate but not overpushed.

skill.notes: Both outputs converge on execution-ready eval documentation and avoid relitigating accepted trade-offs.
